svg.title: Week 1 to Week 6 progression
svg.desc: Six-week progression building a production AWS 3-tier app from Terraform infrastructure to Kubernetes orchestration.

g.text: Week 1 — 3-tier AWS architecture
VPC · ALB across 2 AZs · ASG in private subnets · Multi-AZ RDS · Terraform IaC
Week 2 — Remote state management
S3 bucket with versioning · DynamoDB for state locking (LockID)
terraform init -migrate-state · state verified in S3 · cost $0
Week 3 — GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline
Feature branch → PR → terraform fmt / validate / plan posted to PR
Merge to main → terraform apply → 29 resources created
Zero manual steps end-to-end
Week 4 — Docker containerization + ECR
Dockerized Node.js app · GitHub Actions builds and pushes to ECR
EC2 pulls image via IAM role — no stored credentials
Zero manual deployments · code push to production
Week 5 — CloudWatch monitoring + observability
CloudWatch Agent · 5 log streams (system, app, container, setup)
6 alarms — EC2 CPU, ALB latency, RDS CPU, memory, DB connections
SNS topics for critical and warning alerts · custom dashboard
ASG with health check grace periods · multi-AZ verified
Week 6 — EKS Kubernetes orchestration
EKS cluster + node group (2x t3.micro) provisioned via Terraform
EC2/ASG replaced by Kubernetes Deployment — 2 pods, always running
HPA — auto scales pods 2→6 at 70% CPU · NodePort 30080 → ALB
DB password in Kubernetes Secret · kubectl rollout (~30 sec deploy)
Multi-AZ pods verified · rolling deploy with zero downtime

svg.text: Each week builds on the previous — same VPC, ALB, RDS, and CI/CD pipeline throughout
IaC → State → CI/CD → Containers → Observability → Orchestration
